summary: Single source of truth shared by the two implementations of Uncorked: native SwiftUI (App/, ships to iOS) and the web app (web/, wrapped by Capacitor for Android). Neither shares runtime code; they conform to the shared behavior described here. Run `npm run check:parity` to see whether a platform has drifted or fallen behind.

sharedTruth.algorithm: cycle-vectors.json — golden input->output vectors for the predictor. Both engines MUST reproduce these. Regenerate from the JS engine with `npm run gen:vectors` after an intentional algorithm change, then port the same change to CycleEngine.swift.
sharedTruth.tokens: tokens.json — design tokens (colors, radii, type). The web tokens.css and Swift Theme.swift both mirror these values.
sharedTruth.models: The logged-data enums (Flow, Mood, Symptom, Discharge, Phase) and the DayLog shape must match between web/core/models.js and App/Core/CycleModels.swift. Persistence blob key is `flowtear.state.v1` on both.

platformDivergences.note: Both platforms are at contracts v1.1.0 — no version drift. These are deliberate, permanent differences where the platforms genuinely differ; they are NOT drift and do not need porting.
items: app-lock: iOS says 'Face ID', Android says 'biometrics' (fingerprint / face unlock / screen lock) via @aparajita/capacitor-biometric-auth. Same key (flowtear.appLock), same behaviour.

reminders: iOS uses UNUserNotificationCenter, Android uses @capacitor/local-notifications. Same keys, same copy, same schedule. Capacitor needs numeric notification ids (1001/1002) where Swift uses strings.

cycle-report-csv: identical CSV bytes. iOS shares via ShareLink; Android writes to the app cache (@capacitor/filesystem) and opens the system share sheet (@capacitor/share). A plain browser falls back to a Blob download.
theme presets: tokens.css ships cherry/rose/peony/soft. Swift additionally has pink/light/dark; those need [data-theme] blocks in tokens.css before the web sheet can offer them.
motion: several SwiftUI flourishes (Posey's animations, SparkleBurst) render as calmer static/CSS equivalents on web.

howToStaySynced: 1. Change shared behavior in ONE place first (algorithm in engine.js + CycleEngine.swift, tokens in tokens.json, models in both models files).
2. Bump contractsVersion here, and the matching version stamp in the platform(s) you updated (web/core/version.js and/or App/Core/ContractsVersion.swift).
3. If you changed the algorithm, run `npm run gen:vectors` to refresh cycle-vectors.json.
4. Run `npm run check:parity`. It flags any platform whose stamp is behind contractsVersion, and fails if the web engine no longer matches the golden vectors.
